issues.title: `gogo draft rm <id>` is documented as "delete a draft" but forwards to plan-rm-target (needs a <source>) and always fails
issues.description:
cli/draft.go's draftHelp usage block states: `gogo draft rm [--project

] delete a draft`. But cmdDraft's default case forwards `rm` verbatim to cmdPlanStore (cli/plan.go), whose `rm` verb is planRemove — `gogo plan rm [:]`, which REMOVES A TARGET/MEMBER, requires TWO positional args, and does not delete the plan. Reproduced live: `gogo draft rm plan-704558a9 --project acme` (a bare id, exactly as draftHelp documents) exits 2 with `gogo plan rm: needs [:]` — the documented command never works. The actual way to delete a draft is the UNDOCUMENTED `gogo draft delete ` (confirmed working: `gogo draft delete plan-704558a9 --project acme` -> `deleted plan plan-704558a9`, exit 0). Contrast with cli/epic.go's epicHelp, which correctly documents BOTH `epic rm : ... unlink a work item` (2-arg) and `epic delete ... delete a plan` (1-arg) as distinct verbs — draftHelp only documents one line and picks the wrong verb name/semantics for it. This is the same failure class REV-001 fixed in README.md/SKILL.md (a CLI-facing command that, followed literally, silently errors) but is a NEW, previously-unflagged instance living inside the CLI's own --help text (draft.go), not the external docs REV-001 touched — so REV-001's fix did not catch it and the gate (which only greps verb presence, not behavioural accuracy) does not catch it either. Not data-destructive and not a crash; a user/agent following `gogo draft --help` literally just gets a clean but confusing exit-2 dead end.
issues.proposed_solution:
AGENT-FIXABLE. Fix draftHelp in cli/draft.go to match epicHelp's pattern: remove the false `rm ... delete a draft` line and add the real, already-working verb — `gogo draft delete [--project

] delete a draft` (cmdDraft's default case already forwards `delete` to planDelete correctly; only the documentation is wrong). Since a draft-status plan has no targets/members yet, `draft rm ` (unlink) is not a meaningful operation for a draft the way it is for an epic — so dropping the `rm` line from draftHelp entirely (rather than trying to keep it) is the more honest fix; keep `rm` reachable only via the canonical `gogo plan rm` for anyone who deliberately wants to unlink a target from a plan that happens to be in draft status. Re-run TestCLICommandEnumerationInSync + add/extend a cli test asserting `gogo draft --help` never advertises a subcommand+arity that cmdPlanStore's matching verb rejects.
issues.fix_summary: cli/draft.go: `gogo draft rm <id>` (single id, no <source>) now DELETES the memberless draft — cmdDraft routes `rm`/`remove` to a new draftRemove helper that maps a bare id to planDelete (the plan-delete path) while `draft rm <id> <source>[:<slug>]` still forwards to planRemove (target/member unlink); `gogo plan rm <id> <source>` is unchanged. draftHelp rewritten to match: it documents `draft rm <id> … delete a draft` and `draft delete <id> … (alias of draft rm)`, and no longer implies a <source> arg. epicHelp reviewed — it already documents `epic rm <id> <source>:<slug>` (unlink) and `epic delete <id>` (delete) accurately, so it was left unchanged (no mismatch). Tests: cli/draft_test.go — repurposed TestCmdDraftRmDeletesDraft (asserts `draft rm <id>` deletes) and added TestCmdDraftHelpMatchesBehavior (help advertises both single-id delete verbs, does NOT advertise the 2-arg unlink arity, and both verbs actually delete at that arity). TestCLICommandEnumerationInSync + TestSkillsBashNoUnsafeRm stay green. E2E-verified against the built 0.21.0 binary with isolated dirs: the tester's exact repro `gogo draft rm <id> --project acme` → `deleted plan …`, exit 0 (was exit 2); the 2-arg `draft rm <id> <source>` unlinks and leaves the plan intact.

issues.title: Multiple correlation chips truncate to an indistinguishable `⛓ plan-…` at narrow board-card widths, losing which plan(s) a work item belongs to
issues.description: internal/tui/view.go renderCard (~line 570-584): correlationChipsPlain's output is appended only when `room := width - lipgloss.Width(badgeLine) - 1 >= 8`, then `truncate(plain, room)` (view.go:795, min budget 4) ellipsis-truncates rather than wraps (the comment explains this is deliberate — truncating avoids desyncing the card-height math a wrap would cause). Reproduced hands-on: rendering the real e2e fixture's acme project board at Width=120 (a plausible ~30-col-per-card 4-column layout) showed feature-x's chip render as `⛓ plan-…` (7f3a hash fully lost) and feature-y's SECOND chip (many-to-many) collapse to a bare `⛓…` (plan-b lost entirely) — both plans read identically once truncated, so a viewer cannot tell from the board alone which plan(s) a card belongs to at that width. At a comfortable width (200 cols, matching the project's other pinned tests) both chips render in full (`⛓ plan-7f3a`, `⛓ plan-a ⛓ plan-b`) — verified in the same run. Functionality is unaffected: `#plan-XXXX` filtering operates on the real Feature.Correlations data, not the truncated display string, and the underlying state.md is untouched. This is a legibility nit at the narrow end of the supported width range, not a data-loss or crash bug, and is a DIFFERENT code path from REV-006 (the source-tag truncation fix, which is confirmed still holding).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (polish, non-blocking). Either (a) raise correlationChipsPlain's minimum room budget so a chip only renders when at least the `⛓ plan-` prefix + a few hash chars fit meaningfully (e.g. require room >= 10-12, matching a plan-<hex8> id's practical legibility floor) and omit the chip entirely below that (consistent with the card already omitting an agent chip it can't fit) rather than rendering a content-free ellipsis, or (b) truncate each chip individually to keep at least N trailing hash characters visible (e.g. `⛓ …f3a`) so distinct ids stay visually distinguishable even when tight. Add a table-driven test alongside the existing TestCardRendersTwoCorrelationChips (internal/tui/correlation_test.go) at a narrow width (e.g. 25-30) asserting either omission or a still-distinguishing truncation for two DIFFERENT plan ids.
issues.fix_summary: internal/tui/view.go renderCard: when the full ⛓ plan-<id> chip(s) don't fit the card (lipgloss.Width(plain) > room), the card now renders a compact `⛓ ×N` count fallback (new correlationCountFallback helper) instead of ellipsis-truncating a single id to an indistinguishable `⛓ plan-…`. The card still communicates 'belongs to N plans' at narrow widths; at comfortable widths the full ids render unchanged (binary: full-fit → full ids, else → count). The room>=8 gate and existing truncate safety net are preserved, so card-height math is untouched. Test: internal/tui/correlation_test.go — TestCardNarrowCorrelationChipsCountFallback renders a two-plan card at width 26 (asserts `⛓ ×2` present and neither full id survives) and at width 400 (asserts both full `⛓ plan-…` ids render and no `×2` fallback). Existing TestCardRendersTwoCorrelationChips still green.

issues.title: A plan body containing a literal `--correlation plan-XXXX`-shaped substring is ambiguous input for the gogo-plan skill's correlation-capture step (CLI-side injection-safety confirmed solid)
issues.description: Verified live with a stubbed `claude` on PATH + real tmux (internal/launch's Launch, via `gogo plan promote`): a plan whose title/description itself contained embedded shell metacharacters, quotes, a newline, AND the literal substring `--correlation plan-fake001 lookalike` arrived at the target process as ONE single trailing argv element exactly as documented (confirmed via the stub's recorded argv) — the CLI-side injection-safety guarantee (PlanIntent/launch.go) holds perfectly under real tmux + a real spawned process, not just the unit-test fake launcher. However, the resulting command string then legitimately contains TWO `--correlation` occurrences: the user-authored fake one (mid-body) and the real one launch.PlanIntent appends after the goal (skills/gogo-plan/SKILL.md Step 0: "If it carries an explicit `--correlation plan-XXXX` param... CAPTURE that id"). The skill instruction (read by an LLM, not a strict flag parser) does not state that only the TRAILING/appended occurrence is authoritative, so a plan body containing an earlier lookalike substring is ambiguous for the model executing the skill, risking the wrong plan id being stamped into a spawned work item's state.md. This is speculative (I could not run a real claude session per the test scope — spawn is asserted via the launcher/argv seam only) and requires an unusual plan body (adversarial or accidental) to trigger; worst case is a mislabeled correlation chip, not code execution or data loss.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (defense in depth). Sharpen skills/gogo-plan/SKILL.md Step 0 to state explicitly: honor only a `--correlation plan-XXXX` token that appears as the LAST token of the invocation (the one structurally appended after the goal by the launcher), and treat any other occurrence of the literal string `--correlation` inside the goal text as ordinary prose, never a flag. Optionally, as a belt-and-suspenders CLI-side mitigation, have launch.PlanIntent (internal/launch/launch.go) log or the plan-new command warn when a user-supplied title/description already contains the substring `--correlation` (cheap to detect, avoids ever constructing an ambiguous command in the first place).
issues.fix_summary: skills/gogo-plan/SKILL.md Step 0 (the --correlation parse) now states that the AUTHORITATIVE correlation id is the LAST (trailing) `--correlation plan-XXXX` token — the cockpit CLI always appends it as the FINAL token of the invocation — so a plan BODY that itself contains an earlier `--correlation plan-YYYY` lookalike substring is treated as ordinary prose (never a flag) and only the trailing token is honored. Two sentences added; purely the skill's interpretation guidance (CLI-side injection-safety was already solid). No bash/rm shapes introduced — TestSkillsBashNoUnsafeRm stays green.
